principle: Bricks remains page-design authority. DSA remains Surface/app authority. Theme authors may style/arrange DSA surfaces and may rely on semantic Bricks markers, but must not copy per-site Bricks element IDs.

semanticAttributes.name: data-dsa-bricks-id
semanticAttributes.meaning: Stable runtime marker for observed Bricks element roots. Never hardcode across sites.

semanticAttributes.name: data-dsa-bricks-type
semanticAttributes.meaning: Observed Bricks element type for registry/classification.

semanticAttributes.name: data-dsa-open-module
semanticAttributes.meaning: Canonical Bricks/page launcher target for a registered DSA module such as cart, profile, search, menu, saved, links, theme, ai, notifications, or ios-install.

semanticAttributes.name: data-role="section" / .seam-section
semanticAttributes.meaning: Public Seam section meaning for page structure. Kiwe may read existing sections as menu-context fallback when configured heading TOC items are unavailable; do not add this solely for DSA Menu.

semanticAttributes.name: data-dsa-search-bridge
semanticAttributes.meaning: Bricks Filter Search element that DSA Search may bridge into.

builderControls: Icon element: Kiwe DSA launcher module selector
Filter Search element: Use as DSA Search bridge
WooCommerce mini-cart: quantity stepper and Kiwe mini-cart controls
Product add-to-cart: DSA add-to-cart enhancer controls
Product upsells: linked products / FBT controls

exportsToBricks.variables: Kiwe universal tokens are exported to Bricks global variables as kiwe-* variables.
exportsToBricks.colors: Kiwe universal color tokens are exported to the Bricks color palette as Kiwe Universal.
exportsToBricks.classes: Kiwe Seam framework classes are exported to Bricks global classes under Kiwe Seam categories. Bricks stores names/categories; Kiwe still ships the actual framework CSS so the contract remains portable beyond Bricks.

safeForThemes: Consume Kiwe tokens exported to Bricks variables.
Style DSA surfaces with universal kiwe-* and compatibility dsa-* tokens.
Design Bricks pages that contain semantic DSA launchers.
Use DSA Search bridge when Bricks owns the page query.
forbiddenForThemes: Do not parse or persist Bricks post meta from a theme.
Do not copy generated Bricks IDs between websites.
Do not replace Bricks query authority with DSA theme code.
Do not create alternate mini-cart/checkout/payment authority.
